reasoning: The programmatic precision score is extremely low (0.372), which strongly corroborates the LLM fidelity observation regarding content duplication on page 2. While the content recall is high, the presence of redundant text and full-page images of the same content creates a poor accessibility experience. Given the partial disagreement between signals and the 3x cost penalty for false positives, human judgment is required to determine if the duplication is acceptable.

concerns.analysis: Significant body content is visually duplicated in 'Slide Number 2'. The conversion has extracted the text from PDF page 2 (Title, Date, Legend items, and Sources) and rendered it as HTML text. However, it also renders a full-page image of that same PDF page immediately below the extracted text. This results in the slide title, date, legend, and source attribution appearing twice within the main body of the slide—once as text and once within the image block.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 2 of the PDF contains the title 'NC Parcels Project Status by County', date 'April 9, 2025', legend text, and sources text.
concerns.output_quote: Slide Number 2 in the HTML displays the title, date, legend, and sources as text, followed by an image of the original page that duplicates all of this information.

action_items: Verify if the low precision score is caused strictly by the duplication of page 2 or if there is hallucinated text.
Evaluate if the redundant image of page 2 should be removed or marked as decorative to improve accessibility.
Determine if the conversion of the map/imagery components is legible in the HTML format.
